summary: The explicit sec-e2e-027 entrypoint remains frozen and currently passes, the TTL-downgrade plus full-chain gap-validation semantics remain green, and startup heartbeat registration remains green; however coding delivery no longer matches the implementation architecture contract because canonical-runtime-client-id-003 is reopened. The new live supporting guard proves that one online QwenPaw runtime is still projected as multiple Security Center canonical clients after startup heartbeat plus later session-scoped security traffic, and the implementation architecture must now drive Coding/Repair to collapse those projections into one canonical terminal without false DIVERGED/OPEN fork states.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Within one frozen testcase path, first let a previously trusted device session miss lease heartbeats and attempt denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive a distinct recovery control point followed by a second restored model-access attempt.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The explicit entrypoint must observe a pre-recovery UNTRUSTED-and-denied frame through `pre_recovery_console_status` and a distinct post-recovery trust-restored frame through `post_recovery_console_status`; the second frame must not reuse the denied rejoin timeline or denied console response.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, test-failure-records.json, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, and tests/integration/security/harness.py to confirm sec-e2e-027 remains mounted to the frozen explicit entrypoint with the required business-readable markers.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves acceptanceCriteria, handoff entryPath, failure record path, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN markers, harness method names, and business failure category strings remain frozen for sec-e2e-027.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves sec-e2e-027 is frozen into the runtime security boundary, the separate Security Center deployment boundary, the backend HTTP API lease registry and reconnect gate, the operator web lease-expiry view, and the tests architecture contract.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-client-identity-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read src/qwenpaw/security/audit_foundation.py and deploy/api/store.py to compare the client id used by startup heartbeat against the client ids used by recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves one live runtime must use one canonical Security Center client id across heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection, and that Security Center must not special-case runtime-heartbeat::<fingerprint> as a separate bootstrap-only terminal identity. Current repository state still passes this static guard, so it remains necessary but insufficient.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-regression
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise repository-owned security configuration surfaces while Coding/Repair adds heartbeat and recovery-gate behavior behind the frozen explicit entrypoint.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Configuration regressions are caught without widening the explicit acceptance boundary for sec-e2e-027.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-ttl-lease-downgrade-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Seed an aligned client into Security Center state with lease timing that has already expired, then read the cloud-side timeline without invoking any prompt-scripted edge lockdown path.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The timeline must expose last heartbeat timing, computed lease expiry, lease TTL, autonomous UNTRUSTED downgrade, and recovery_required=true. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-full-gap-validation-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Seed an UNTRUSTED client with an open recovery gate, then call recovery_handshake through the explicit-gap-verification trace path using only hash, sequence, and anchor identifiers but no full gap proof.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Recovery must remain required and trust must not return to ALIGNED until the cloud mirror validates a full-chain missing-gap proof. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: runtime-startup-background-heartbeat-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Start a real QwenPaw runtime and observe Security Center before any user prompt mentions lease warmup or lease expiry.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security Center must already show at least one client lease registration with nonzero last_heartbeat_at and lease_expires_at so active defense does not depend on prompt-scripted warmup wording. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-single-online-runtime-identity-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Start a real QwenPaw runtime, allow startup heartbeat registration, then drive one session-scoped lease warmup through the live console without forking the runtime.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security Center must still project exactly one canonical terminal for that online runtime and must not show a false local-hash `DIVERGED`/`OPEN` recovery gate when no fork point exists. Current repository state is expected to fail because overview still shows separate runtime-heartbeat and runtime-session canonical clients.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if sec-e2e-027 loses TTL-driven UNTRUSTED downgrade, split pre/post recovery observations, cloud-validated recovery gate semantics, startup background heartbeat registration semantics, or the single-terminal identity boundary between the edge runtime and Security Center.
codingTargets.nextAction: Treat the explicit sec-e2e-027 path as a read-only acceptance baseline and preserve its dual-frame observations, startup heartbeat registration semantics, and canonical single-terminal Security Center identity.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if Security Center overview ever returns to zero registered lease clients at runtime startup before any user prompt is sent.
codingTargets.nextAction: Preserve the product background Heartbeat Emitter so runtime startup continues to register an initial lease heartbeat automatically and periodic emission remains live while the edge runtime is alive.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if startup heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, restored-access projection, or timeline reads ever diverge back to different Security Center client ids for the same live runtime.
codingTargets.nextAction: Preserve one canonical runtime client id seam in src/qwenpaw/security and keep Security Center alias resolution aligned with that seam.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Current failure signal: one online runtime is still projected as two canonical Security Center terminals after startup heartbeat plus later session-scoped security traffic, which reopens canonical-runtime-client-id-003.
codingTargets.nextAction: Collapse startup heartbeat and later session-scoped security flows onto one canonical Security Center terminal, keep session ids as metadata or display aliases only, and prevent false DIVERGED/OPEN bootstrap-shadow mismatches for live non-forked runtimes.

taskExecutionPlan.executionStrategy: Coding delivery is not yet accepted against the current implementation architecture. Keep sec-e2e-027 on its one frozen explicit entrypoint with two sequential control points and two distinct console observation points, preserve the already-green TTL-driven lease downgrade plus full-chain cloud validation semantics and startup-heartbeat registration semantics, and repair the reopened canonical-runtime-client-id-003 gap until the new live supporting guard plus the static identity guard both pass.

tasks.title: Collapse runtime to one terminal
tasks.objective: Unify startup heartbeat, session-scoped recovery preflight, lockdown projection, restored-access projection, and Security Center timeline reads onto one canonical Security Center terminal per live runtime so one online runtime does not appear as multiple cloud terminals or a false DIVERGED fork.
steps: Replace the current split between runtime-heartbeat::<fingerprint> and runtime-session::<workspace>::<session> with one repository-owned canonical runtime terminal id that is reused by startup heartbeat, recovery preflight, lockdown projection, restored-access projection, and Security Center timeline reads.

Keep browser or session ids only as audit metadata or display aliases, and ensure deploy/api overview continues to expose at most one canonical client for one live runtime even after later session-scoped security traffic.

Align bootstrap shadow initialization and later audit-head comparison so a live non-forked runtime cannot be projected as local_hash_mismatch plus recovery_gate_status OPEN unless a real continuity failure, missing gap, or lease expiry actually occurred.

Do not rewrite the frozen sec-e2e-027 explicit entrypoint, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN body, or the split pre_recovery_console_status/post_recovery_console_status observation contract while repairing this behavior.
tasks.completionSignal: tests/contract/security/test_lease_recovery_semantics_contract.py::test_security_center_projects_one_online_runtime_as_one_canonical_terminal and tests/architecture/security-runtime-client-identity-boundary.test.js both pass, and one live runtime no longer appears as multiple Security Center clients or false DIVERGED fork states while online.

openGaps: canonical-runtime-client-id-003 remains open: the live supporting guard shows one online runtime still projects as multiple Security Center canonical clients after startup heartbeat plus later session-scoped security traffic, and Coding/Repair must converge that flow to one canonical terminal without false DIVERGED/OPEN bootstrap-shadow mismatches.
